step_by_step_thinking: The percentage of SSI (Surgical Site Infection) rates in patients with a clean-contaminated wound typically ranges from studies conducted in different settings. Clean-contaminated wounds include cases where there has been contamination due to manipulation of viscera but no actual spillage of gut contents during surgery. Studies suggest that the incidence of SSIs in such cases generally lies between 1% to 2%. Therefore, considering the available data:
raw_response:
The percentage of SSI (Surgical Site Infection) rates in patients with a clean-contaminated wound typically ranges from studies conducted in different settings. Clean-contaminated wounds include cases where there has been contamination due to manipulation of viscera but no actual spillage of gut contents during surgery. Studies suggest that the incidence of SSIs in such cases generally lies between 1% to 2%. Therefore, considering the available data:
\boxed{A}
